quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 10
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A high-performance research vehicle starting from rest travels along a straight test track with a constant acceleration of $5.40\text{ m/s}^2$. Calculate the total linear distance the vehicle covers during the time interval it takes to reach a velocity of $36.0\text{ m/s}$.

A telecommunications satellite project timeline tracks a subsystem durability cycle that spans exactly $0.00350\text{ weeks}$. Convert this total operational timeframe directly into standard seconds ($\text{s}$).

A warehouse forklift lifts a pallet of heavy machinery vertically upward at a constant speed of $0.750\text{ m/s}$. Analyze the nature of the work performed on the machinery by the upward normal force exerted by the forklift prongs during this upward displacement.
questions.explanation: The normal force acts upward and displacement is upward. Force and displacement are in the same direction — positive work.

questions.question: A $0.580\text{ kg}$ calibration ball is attached to a vertical spring mechanism. When the ball is thrown straight upward, it possesses exactly $145\text{ J}$ of kinetic energy at the instant it leaves the launcher. Calculate the linear speed of the ball at this exact position.

A $0.00420\text{ kg}$ synthetic dart is loaded into a spring-powered test chamber. The compressed internal mechanism expands over $0.130\text{ m}$, exerting an average forward force of $64.0\text{ N}$ on the dart before it exits the chamber. Use energy principles to determine the exit velocity of the dart, assuming it starts from rest.

A $1,450\text{ kg}$ test chassis rolling forward along a straight tracking line at $12.5\text{ m/s}$ experiences a head-on impact test. The barrier exerts a massive retarding force that brings the vehicle to a complete stop in $0.0820\text{ s}$. Calculate the magnitude of the average force sustained by the vehicle during this impact.

An electric industrial winch mechanism hoists a structural load horizontally across a high-friction factory floor at a constant speed of $0.650\text{ m/s}$. The winch must continuously maintain a steady tension force of $4,800\text{ N}$ to overcome sliding friction. Calculate the power output delivered by the system.

A prototype ventilation fan motor consumes $2,400\text{ J}$ of total electrical energy during an operational trial. The system experiences internal electrical resistance losses that dissipate $720\text{ J}$ as waste heat. Calculate the net operating efficiency percentage of the fan motor.

A $2,400\text{ kg}$ transport van moving due north at $14.0\text{ m/s}$ collides with a stationary $1,100\text{ kg}$ compact sedan at an intersection. The two vehicles lock bumpers and slide together as a single mass. Determine the common velocity magnitude of the entangled wreckage right after the collision.

A worker utilizes a second-class lever tool to lift a heavy structural beam. The distance from the pivot fulcrum to the handles where the worker applies an effort force of $220\text{ N}$ is $1.60\text{ m}$. The machine setup exhibits an actual mechanical advantage (AMA) of $4.50$. Calculate the weight magnitude of the load force being lifted.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on impact force calculations, power from force and velocity, and energy methods.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
